description:
A data–science colleague left you a detailed processing log at
    /home/user/project/cleaning.log.  
    It contains one line per action that was taken while tidying a
    small sales dataset.  Each line follows this exact structure  
        YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS,mmm INFO <message>  

    where <message> is one of the following patterns:

        • “Removed duplicate row id=<num>”  
        • “Replaced null price in row id=<num> with mean=<float>”  
        • “Replaced null quantity in row id=<num> with mean=<float>”

    Your task is pure log-analysis; do NOT edit the original log file.

    1. Read /home/user/project/cleaning.log.  
    2. Count how many times each type of action occurred.  
       The three counters you must extract are:  
         a. duplicates_removed  
         b. price_missing_filled  
         c. quantity_missing_filled  
    3. Also record the grand total of log lines (total_operations).  
    4. Create a new file /home/user/project/cleaning_summary.json that contains
       exactly the following four top-level keys and nothing else:

          {
            "duplicates_removed": <int>,
            "price_missing_filled": <int>,
            "quantity_missing_filled": <int>,
            "total_operations": <int>
          }

       • Use 2-space indentation.  
       • All keys must appear in the above order.  
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline.  
    5. Accomplish the task using standard Linux shell utilities
       (e.g. grep, awk, wc); the whole job should realistically take
       5–10 commands.

    When you’re done, the automated checker will verify:

        • /home/user/project/cleaning_summary.json exists.  
        • Its JSON structure and numbers are exactly as specified.  
        • The original /home/user/project/cleaning.log is untouched.

    No other output or files are required.  Good luck!
truth:
Initial state: the directory /home/user/project/ already exists and is
    writable by the non-root user.  It contains exactly one file before the
    task starts:

        Path: /home/user/project/cleaning.log  
        Permissions: 0644  
        Contents (the eight lines shown below, each ending with “\n”):

2023-09-01 09:15:21,123 INFO Removed duplicate row id=3
2023-09-01 09:15:22,456 INFO Replaced null price in row id=2 with mean=1.07
2023-09-01 09:15:23,789 INFO Replaced null quantity in row id=4 with mean=8.25
2023-09-01 09:15:24,012 INFO Replaced null price in row id=5 with mean=1.07
2023-09-01 09:15:25,345 INFO Removed duplicate row id=7
2023-09-01 09:15:26,678 INFO Replaced null price in row id=8 with mean=1.07
2023-09-01 09:15:27,901 INFO Replaced null quantity in row id=9 with mean=8.25
2023-09-01 09:15:29,234 INFO Replaced null quantity in row id=10 with mean=8.25

    Expected final state after the agent finishes:

        Path: /home/user/project/cleaning_summary.json  
        Permissions: 0644 (default touch behaviour is fine)  
        Exact contents (including newlines and 2-space indentation):

{
  "duplicates_removed": 2,
  "price_missing_filled": 3,
  "quantity_missing_filled": 3,
  "total_operations": 8
}

        The original /home/user/project/cleaning.log must remain byte-for-byte
        identical to the initial contents shown above.
